Title: Category Navigation - Main Menu
Preconditions: User logged in; catalog populated
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Hover over main menu navigation
[WAIT] Wait for category dropdown to appear
[ASSERT] Verify dropdown is visible
[ACT] Click on a top-level category
[WAIT] Wait for category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify category page content
Assertions:
[DOM] Main menu displays top-level categories
[DOM] Subcategory items visible on hover
[NAV] Category page loads with correct URL slug
[DOM] Category heading matches selected category
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET category page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Spinner >5s; dropdown not appearing; category page returns 4xx; console TypeError

Title: Category Page - Product Grid View
Preconditions: Category with products exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click 'Grid view' icon
[WAIT] Wait for layout to update
[ASSERT] Verify product cards display in grid
Assertions:
[DOM] Products shown in multi-column grid layout
[DOM] Each card shows image, name, and price
[DOM] Quantity stepper (+) present on each card
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on category page request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Products not displaying; grid layout broken; images not loading; console TypeError

Title: Category Page - Product List View
Preconditions: Category with products exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click 'List view' icon
[WAIT] Wait for layout to update
[ASSERT] Verify list layout renders
Assertions:
[DOM] Products shown in single-column list format
[DOM] More detail visible per product compared to grid
[DOM] Quantity stepper (+) accessible in list view
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on layout toggle request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: List layout not switching; products missing in list view; actions inaccessible; console TypeError

Title: Category - Sorting Options
Preconditions: Category with multiple products
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Open sort dropdown
[ASSERT] Verify sort options are present
[ACT] select 'Price: Low to High' from sort dropdown
[WAIT] Wait for products to reorder
[ASSERT] Verify ascending price order
[ACT] select 'Price: High to Low' from sort dropdown
[WAIT] Wait for products to reorder
[ASSERT] Verify descending price order
[NAV] Click to page 2 of pagination
[ASSERT] Verify sort order persists on page 2
Assertions:
[DOM] Sort dropdown visible with multiple options
[STATE] Products reorder correctly for each sort selection
[MATH] 'Price: Low to High' — first product price <= last product price
[MATH] 'Price: High to Low' — first product price >= last product price
[STATE] Sort state persists across pagination
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on sort change request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Sort dropdown not rendering; products not reordering; sort not persisting on pagination; console TypeError

Title: Brands Page Loads
Preconditions: Store has brand data configured; brands page enabled
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/Brands
[WAIT] Wait for page to load completely
[ASSERT] Verify brand list or grid is displayed
[ASSERT] Check browser console for JavaScript errors
Assertions:
[NAV] /Brands page loads without errors (HTTP 200)
[DOM] Brand list or grid displayed
[DOM] Each brand entry shows brand name (and logo/image if configured)
[DOM] Page title or heading confirms Brands context
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET /Brands returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Brands page returns 4xx/5xx; brand list empty when brands exist; brand images failing to load; console TypeError

Title: Brand Navigation
Preconditions: Brands page loaded; at least one brand with products exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/Brands
[ASSERT] Verify at least one brand entry is visible
[ACT] Click on a brand entry
[WAIT] Wait for brand product listing page to load
[ASSERT] Verify page heading or breadcrumb shows selected brand name
[ASSERT] Verify all listed products belong to the selected brand
Assertions:
[NAV] Clicking brand navigates to a brand-filtered product listing page
[DOM] Page heading or breadcrumb shows selected brand name
[STATE] All displayed products associated with the selected brand
[DOM] Facet filters available on brand result page
[STATE] URL reflects brand filter context
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET brand listing page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Brand entry not clickable; brand listing page returns 4xx; non-brand products appearing in brand listing; console TypeError

Title: Brand Product Count
Preconditions: Brands page loaded; at least one brand entry is visible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/Brands
[ASSERT] Locate a brand entry showing a product count badge or label
[ASSERT] Note the displayed count value
[ACT] Click on that brand
[WAIT] Wait for brand product listing page
[ASSERT] Count actual product cards on the listing page
[ASSERT] Verify the displayed count on the brand entry matches the actual product card count on the listing page
Assertions:
[MATH] Product count shown on brand entry equals number of product cards on the brand listing page
[DOM] Brand entry shows a non-zero product count label
[STATE] Zero-count brands hidden or marked as unavailable if feature configured
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET brand listing returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Product count on Brands page does not match listing count; brand count not showing; console TypeError

Title: PDP SEO - Meta Tags and Open Graph Tags
Preconditions: Any product with SEO info configured (meta title and description set in admin) exists in the B2B virtual catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any product detail page on {{FRONT_URL}} (resolve any in-stock product slug at runtime)
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to fully render
[ACT] Open browser DevTools Elements panel
[ASSERT] Inspect <head> section for meta tags
[ASSERT] Verify meta title tag is present and non-empty
[ASSERT] Verify meta description tag is present and non-empty
[ASSERT] Verify canonical URL tag is present and points to correct PDP URL
[ASSERT] Verify og:title, og:description, og:image, og:url tags are present
[ASSERT] Verify og:image points to a valid product image URL
Assertions:
[DOM] <title> or meta[name=title] is present and non-empty
[DOM] meta[name=description] is present with meaningful content (not empty or placeholder)
[DOM] link[rel=canonical] present with href matching current PDP URL (no query params)
[DOM] meta[property=og:title] present and non-empty
[DOM] meta[property=og:description] present and non-empty
[DOM] meta[property=og:image] present with non-empty image URL
[DOM] meta[property=og:url] present and matches canonical URL
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] og:image URL returns HTTP 200 (image is accessible)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Meta title missing or showing generic site title; meta description empty; canonical URL absent or pointing to wrong page; og:tags missing; og:image returning 404; console TypeError

Title: Category Page SEO - JSON-LD BreadcrumbList and Canonical URL
Preconditions: Category page with at least one level of subcategory hierarchy exists
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any subcategory page on {{FRONT_URL}} (a category with parent > child structure)
[WAIT] Wait for page to fully render
[ACT] Open browser DevTools Elements panel
[ASSERT] Inspect <head> for canonical URL
[ASSERT] Search for <script type=application/ld+json> in page source
[ASSERT] Verify JSON-LD contains @type: BreadcrumbList
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb items match the visible breadcrumb trail on page
[ASSERT] Verify each breadcrumb item has name and item (URL) properties
Assertions:
[DOM] link[rel=canonical] present with href matching current category URL
[DOM] JSON-LD script tag present with @type: BreadcrumbList
[DOM] BreadcrumbList itemListElement array has correct number of items matching visible breadcrumbs
[DOM] Each itemListElement has @type: ListItem, position (integer), name, and item (URL)
[DOM] Breadcrumb positions are sequential starting from 1
[FORMAT] JSON-LD is valid JSON (no parse errors)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET category page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no JSON parse errors from structured data
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Canonical URL missing or pointing to wrong category; JSON-LD absent from page; BreadcrumbList items not matching visible breadcrumbs; JSON-LD parse errors; console TypeError

Title: Multi-Level Category Navigation with Breadcrumbs
Preconditions: Category hierarchy exists with at least 3 levels (top-level > subcategory > sub-subcategory)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}} main catalog page
[ACT] Click on a top-level category in the navigation menu
[WAIT] Wait for top-level category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category]
[ASSERT] Verify URL slug matches category name
[ACT] Click on a subcategory within the top-level category
[WAIT] Wait for subcategory page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level] > [Subcategory]
[ASSERT] Verify URL updates to include subcategory slug
[ACT] Click on a sub-subcategory if available
[WAIT] Wait for sub-subcategory page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level] > [Sub] > [Sub-Sub]
[ACT] Click the top-level breadcrumb link
[WAIT] Wait for navigation
[ASSERT] Verify navigation back to top-level category page
Assertions:
[DOM] Breadcrumb trail updates correctly at each navigation level
[NAV] URL slug updates to reflect current category hierarchy at each level
[DOM] Category heading matches the currently selected category at each level
[DOM] Each breadcrumb item is a clickable link (except the current page)
[NAV] Clicking a breadcrumb ancestor navigates to that level
[DOM] Products displayed are relevant to the current category level
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET each category page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Breadcrumbs not updating at deeper levels; URL not reflecting category hierarchy; breadcrumb links not navigable; heading mismatching current category; console TypeError

Title: Empty Category Handling - No Products Message
Preconditions: A category exists with zero products assigned (or all products are deactivated/out of stock)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a category page with no products on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for page to fully render
[ASSERT] Verify a 'No products found' or equivalent empty state message is displayed
[ASSERT] Verify the page does NOT show a blank/empty content area with no explanation
[ASSERT] Verify the filter panel is hidden or shows no filter options
[ASSERT] Verify pagination controls are not displayed
[ASSERT] Check browser console for JavaScript errors
Assertions:
[DOM] Empty state message visible (e.g., 'No products found' or 'No items in this category')
[DOM] No blank page — user understands why no products are shown
[DOM] Filter panel is either hidden or disabled (no filterable values)
[DOM] No pagination controls displayed for empty result set
[DOM] Category heading still shows correct category name
[NAV] Breadcrumbs still functional for navigation back
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET category page returns HTTP 200 (not 4xx)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
[CONSOLE] no Vue hydration warnings
Failure_Signals: Blank page with no message; filter panel showing options for zero products; pagination showing for empty set; JS errors on empty category; console TypeError

Title: All Products Dropdown - Displays All Top-Level Categories
Preconditions: Storefront populated with multiple top-level categories; main navigation rendered
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Locate 'All Products' or equivalent top-level navigation item in the main menu
[ACT] Hover over or click 'All Products' to open the dropdown
[WAIT] Wait for the dropdown to fully render
[ASSERT] Verify the dropdown is visible and lists category items
[ASSERT] Verify at least one category link is present in the dropdown
[ASSERT] Verify each category item has a text label and a clickable href
Assertions:
[DOM] 'All Products' dropdown is visible after hover/click
[DOM] At least one top-level category link is displayed in the dropdown
[DOM] Each category item shows a non-empty text label
[DOM] Each category item has a non-empty href pointing to a category URL path
[STATE] Dropdown does not show blank or placeholder items
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET {{FRONT_URL}} returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on menu data request
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors

Title: All Products Dropdown - Category Link Navigates to Correct Category Page
Preconditions: Storefront populated; at least one top-level category accessible from 'All Products' dropdown
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Hover over or click 'All Products' in the main navigation to open the dropdown
[WAIT] Wait for dropdown to appear
[ASSERT] Identify any category in the dropdown (note its label)
[ACT] Click the identified category link
[WAIT] Wait for the category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify the URL contains a category slug
[ASSERT] Verify the page heading matches the clicked category name
[ASSERT] Verify the breadcrumb shows the correct category hierarchy
Assertions:
[NAV] URL after click contains a category slug matching the label clicked
[DOM] Page heading matches the category name selected from the dropdown
[DOM] Breadcrumbs show: Home > [Category Name]
[DOM] Product grid or product listing is rendered on the category page
[STATE] Active/highlighted state on the category in navigation (if applicable)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET category page returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors

Title: Category SEO Slug URL Resolves Correctly
Preconditions: A category with a configured SEO-friendly slug exists in the catalog
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category page using its SEO slug URL (resolve a category slug at runtime from the navigation menu)
[WAIT] Wait for the page to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify page loads with HTTP 200 (no redirect to an error page)
[ASSERT] Verify the URL remains as the slug URL (no redirect to an ID-based URL)
[ASSERT] Verify the category heading is non-empty
[ASSERT] Verify products are listed on the category page
[ACT] Open browser DevTools and check the link[rel=canonical] tag value
[ASSERT] Verify the canonical URL matches the slug URL
Assertions:
[NAV] Slug URL resolves to the correct category page without redirect to an ID-based URL
[DOM] Category heading is non-empty
[DOM] Products are displayed on the page
[DOM] link[rel=canonical] href equals the current slug URL (not an ID-based URL)
[STATE] No 301/302 redirect from slug to ID URL
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET slug URL returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Slug URL returning 404; slug redirecting to an ID-based URL; canonical pointing to a different URL; heading empty; console TypeError

Title: Catalog Listing Page Renders Correctly at 768px Tablet Viewport
Preconditions: Category with products exists; storefront accessible
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any category listing page on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Resize the browser viewport to 768x1024 (tablet portrait)
[WAIT] Wait for layout to reflow
[ASSERT] Verify the product grid adapts to the tablet viewport (typically 2-column grid at 768px)
[ASSERT] Verify product card images names and prices are fully visible with no overflow or clipping
[ASSERT] Verify the navigation menu is accessible (hamburger or condensed menu)
[ASSERT] Verify facet/filter panel is accessible (collapsed drawer or sidebar condensed)
[SCROLL] Scroll down to verify entire product listing is accessible
[ASSERT] Verify pagination controls are visible and functional
Assertions:
[DOM] Product grid displays in tablet-appropriate layout at 768px
[DOM] Product cards are fully visible with no image cropping text overflow or price truncation
[DOM] Navigation accessible at 768px
[DOM] Facet/filter controls accessible (not off-screen)
[DOM] Pagination controls visible and not overlapping content
[STATE] No horizontal scroll bar visible at 768px width
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on category page at tablet viewport
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Product grid overflowing at 768px; product cards clipped or inaccessible; navigation menu not accessible; horizontal scrollbar visible; pagination hidden off-screen; console TypeError

Title: Breadcrumb Resets When Navigating from Subcategory Back to Parent via Main Menu
Preconditions: Category hierarchy exists with at least 2 levels; user can navigate subcategory then return to parent via main menu
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click on a top-level category in the main navigation menu
[WAIT] Wait for top-level category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category]
[ACT] Click on a subcategory link within the top-level category page
[WAIT] Wait for subcategory page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory]
[ACT] Click on the top-level category link in the main navigation menu (not the breadcrumb)
[WAIT] Wait for top-level category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify URL updated to top-level category URL (subcategory segment absent)
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] (subcategory name must be absent)
[ASSERT] Verify category heading matches top-level category name
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb trail contains exactly 2 items: Home + Top-Level Category
[DOM] subcategory name absent from breadcrumb after navigating to parent
[NAV] URL matches top-level category URL (no subcategory slug)
[DOM] category page heading matches top-level category name
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no Vue hydration warnings or TypeError
[NETWORK] GET top-level category page returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx on navigation
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb still shows subcategory name after navigating to parent; URL correct but breadcrumb stale; heading correct but breadcrumb wrong (VCST-4728 regression check)

Title: Breadcrumb Resets When Using Browser Back Button from Subcategory to Parent
Preconditions: Category hierarchy with at least 2 levels; user on subcategory page reached via forward navigation
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}
[ACT] Click on a top-level category in the main navigation menu
[WAIT] Wait for top-level category page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category]
[ACT] Click on a subcategory
[WAIT] Wait for subcategory page to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory]
[ACT] Press browser Back button
[WAIT] Wait for browser to navigate to previous page (top-level category)
[ASSERT] Verify URL is the top-level category URL
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category]
[ASSERT] Verify category heading matches top-level category
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb contains exactly 2 items: Home + Top-Level Category
[DOM] subcategory name absent from breadcrumb
[NAV] URL is top-level category URL
[DOM] heading matches top-level category name
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no hydration errors or TypeError after back navigation
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx triggered by back navigation
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb still shows subcategory name after browser Back; URL correct but breadcrumb stale — direct reproduction of VCST-4728

Title: Breadcrumb Reflects Correct Path When Navigating Between Sibling Subcategories
Preconditions: Category hierarchy with one parent and at least 2 sibling subcategories
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to any subcategory page (e.g., Home > [Parent] > [Subcategory A])
[WAIT] Wait for page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Parent] > [Subcategory A]
[ACT] Click on the parent category in the main navigation menu
[WAIT] Wait for parent category page to load
[ACT] Click on a different sibling subcategory (e.g., [Subcategory B])
[WAIT] Wait for sibling subcategory page to load
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Parent] > [Subcategory B]
[ASSERT] Verify [Subcategory A] is absent from the breadcrumb
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb shows Home > [Parent] > [New Sibling] — no trace of previous sibling
[DOM] breadcrumb has exactly 3 items matching the new navigation path
[NAV] URL reflects the new sibling subcategory slug
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or hydration warnings
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb shows blend of both paths; previous subcategory name persists; breadcrumb shows 4 items instead of 3; heading correct but breadcrumb wrong

Title: Breadcrumb Determinism on Rapid Forward-Back-Forward Navigation
Preconditions: Category hierarchy with at least 2 levels; user can navigate quickly
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to a top-level category on {{FRONT_URL}}
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category]
[ACT] Click into a subcategory
[WAIT] Wait for page to load
[ACT] Click the top-level category in main navigation
[WAIT] Wait for page to load
[ACT] Click the same subcategory again
[WAIT] Wait 1 second for all navigation and state to settle
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb shows: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory] (final destination)
[ASSERT] Verify breadcrumb does not show intermediate stale state
[ASSERT] Verify URL matches subcategory
Assertions:
[DOM] breadcrumb shows 3 items: Home > [Top-Level Category] > [Subcategory] matching final destination
[DOM] no intermediate stale breadcrumb state visible
[NAV] URL is subcategory URL
[DOM] heading matches subcategory name
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[CONSOLE] no race condition errors or uncaught exceptions
[NETWORK] no 4xx/5xx
Failure_Signals: breadcrumb flickers between states; breadcrumb shows parent instead of subcategory after rapid navigation; heading correct but breadcrumb stale

Title: Configurable Product PDP - Required Sections Present
Preconditions: A configurable product with multiple configuration sections (Product, Text, File types) exists in the B2B virtual catalog; resolve a configurable product slug at runtime via xAPI products query with filter='productType:Configurable'
Steps:
[NAV] Resolve any configurable product slug at runtime: query xAPI products(storeId,filter='productType:Configurable') — pick first result's slug; navigate to that product's PDP on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for PDP to fully load
[ASSERT] Verify product name is displayed in the H1/title element (non-empty string)
[ASSERT] Verify price is displayed and is > 0 (not zero or placeholder)
[ASSERT] Verify product description section is visible
[ASSERT] Verify configuration sections are rendered below product info
[ASSERT] Verify each required configuration section shows a label and selection options
[ASSERT] Verify 'Customize' or 'Configure' call-to-action is present
[SCROLL] Scroll down to verify all sections are rendered
[ASSERT] Verify total price updates as configuration options are selected
Assertions: [DOM] Product name displayed in H1 or title element (non-empty string) [DOM] Base price displayed and > 0 (matches /^\$[0-9,]+\.[0-9]{2}$/ pattern) [DOM] Configuration sections rendered with labels matching admin-defined section names [DOM] Required sections marked with required indicator (asterisk or similar) [DOM] Optional sections show 'None' as a selectable option [DOM] Product images loaded (at least one with non-empty src) [STATE] Total price dynamically updates when options are selected
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (productConfiguration) returns HTTP 200
[NETWORK] All image requests return HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Configuration sections not rendering; price showing $0; required sections not marked; options not loading; total price not updating; console TypeError

Title: Configurable Product - Variation Selection Updates Price and SKU
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
[PRE:RESET_CART]
A configurable product with a Variation section exists in the B2B virtual catalog; the product has at least 2 variation options; resolve the product slug at runtime via xAPI products query with filter='productType:Configurable' and hasVariations:true
Steps:
[NAV] Resolve a configurable product with variations at runtime: query xAPI products(storeId,filter='productType:Configurable') — pick a result that has hasVariations=true; navigate to its PDP on {{FRONT_URL}}
[WAIT] Wait for PDP and configuration sections to load
[ASSERT] Verify the variation configuration section is present (e.g., a section listing variant options)
[ACT] Select the first variation option from the section
[WAIT] Wait for price and details to update
[ASSERT] Verify price updates to reflect the selected variation's price (price > 0)
[ASSERT] Verify SKU or variant identifier updates to a non-empty value
[ACT] Select a second different variation option
[WAIT] Wait for price and details to update
[ASSERT] Verify price changes (or stays the same if variations share a price — both valid; assert price > 0)
[ASSERT] Verify SKU updates to the second variation's identifier
[ACT] Click 'Add to cart' button (configurable PDP uses explicit Add to cart, not the qty stepper)
[WAIT] Wait for cart confirmation
[ASSERT] Verify cart shows the configured product with a non-empty variation identifier and price > 0
Assertions:
[DOM] Variation section displays available options from the product's variations
[STATE] Selecting a variation updates the displayed price (price > 0 matching /^\$[0-9,]+\.[0-9]{2}$/)
[STATE] SKU or variant label changes on selection (non-empty value)
[DOM] Cart line item shows configured product with non-empty variation details
[STATE] Cart total > 0 and reflects the configured product's price
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] POST /graphql (productConfiguration) returns correct variation data
[NETWORK] POST add-to-cart returns HTTP 200
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors
Failure_Signals: Variation section not showing options; price not updating on selection (stays at $0); wrong or empty SKU displayed; cart showing base product instead of configured variant; console TypeError
Cleanup: Remove configured product from cart after test

Title: Category Permalink Integrity — EN Locale Baseline Slug Capture
Preconditions:
[PRE:SIGNIN_AS:USER_DEFAULT]
Storefront accessible at {{FRONT_URL}}; at least one top-level category visible in the main navigation; EN is the active locale (default)
Steps:
[NAV] Navigate to {{FRONT_URL}}/catalog
[WAIT] Category listing page loads; at least one category card/link visible
[ACT] Click the first top-level category link in the category grid
[WAIT] Category page loads; URL settles (no redirect)
[ASSERT] Capture the current URL path segment after {{FRONT_URL}} — this is the EN-locale slug (e.g. '/accessories') — store as SLUG_EN
[ASSERT] Verify SLUG_EN is non-empty and does not start with a language code prefix (/de/, /fr/, /en-US/, etc.)
[ACT] Inspect page heading or breadcrumb — note the category name displayed
[ASSERT] Verify category page renders correctly: product grid visible OR empty-state message visible (not a 404)
Assertions:
[NAV] URL is {{FRONT_URL}}<SLUG_EN> (the slug matches the category, no extraneous characters)
[DOM] Category name heading is visible and non-empty
[DOM] Page is not a 404 — no 'page not found' heading or error content
[STATE] SLUG_EN captured is the full unmodified path segment (starts with '/', no language prefix)
Cross_Layer_Checks:
[NETWORK] GET {{FRONT_URL}}<SLUG_EN> returns HTTP 200 (no 404/301 to error page)
[CONSOLE] no TypeError or JS errors on category page load
Failure_Signals: Category page returns 404; SLUG_EN is empty; URL redirected to /catalog or homepage unexpectedly; console TypeError on page load
Cleanup: none — read-only navigation; no state change
